title: sitobolium cicutarium
intro: Sitobolium cicutarium, also known as hoary cress, is a biennial or perennial plant in the mustard family that is native to Europe and Asia but has been introduced to other parts of the world as an invasive species. The plant has been used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds. The health benefits of Sitobolium cicutarium include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
